puzzle.title: The Trial of Thirst
puzzle.intro: A community, yearning for leadership to defend their cause, petitioned for a commander to be appointed over them. When their request was answered, the chosen one was met with skepticism, for this individual did not possess the worldly status they expected. Before the ultimate confrontation, this new leader would test the very discipline of the army with a simple, yet profound, trial of restraint.
clues: I was appointed as a commander after my people demanded a leader to fight in the cause of Allah.
Many of my own people initially questioned my appointment, citing my lack of wealth and noble lineage.
I was chosen because Allah had granted me an abundance of knowledge and a powerful physical stature.
A clear sign of my divine appointment was the return of the Ark of the Covenant, carried by angels, which brought peace to the hearts of the believers.
As I marched forth with my soldiers, I announced that we would be tested by a river.
I commanded my army that whoever drank deeply from the river was not of my company, while those who only took a single scoop in their hand would remain.

identity.label: Who Am I?
options: Jalut (Goliath)
Talut (Saul)
Prophet Dawud
Dhul-Qarnayn
Namrud (Nimrod)

trial.label: What Was My Main Trial?
options: Building a barrier against Gog and Magog
Facing a giant in single combat
Testing an army's obedience at a river
Leading an army of jinn and birds
Surviving being thrown into a fire
trial.answer: Testing an army's obedience at a river

location.label: What Was a Key Sign of My Authority?
options: A staff that turned into a serpent
A camel that emerged from a rock
A coat of mail made from softened iron
The Ark of the Covenant (At-Tabut)
A table of food from the heavens

outcome.label: What Was the Outcome of My Campaign?
options: Defeating the army of Jalut (Goliath)
Parting the Red Sea to escape a tyrant
Establishing a long-lasting dynasty in my name
Building the sacred Kaaba in Makkah
Being raised to the heavens without dying

puzzle.arabic: فَلَمَّا فَصَلَ طَالُوتُ بِٱلْجُنُودِ قَالَ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ مُبْتَلِيكُم بِنَهَرٍۢ فَمَن شَرِبَ مِنْهُ فَلَيْسَ مِنِّى وَمَن لَّمْ يَطْعَمْهُ فَإِنَّهُۥ مِنِّىٓ إِلَّا مَنِ ٱغْتَرَفَ غُرْفَةًۢ بِيَدِهِۦ ۚ فَشَرِبُوا۟ مِنْهُ إِلَّا قَلِيلًۭا مِّنْهُمْ ۚ فَلَمَّا جَاوَزَهُۥ هُوَ وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ مَعَهُۥ قَالُوا۟ لَا طَاقَةَ لَنَا ٱلْيَوْمَ بِجَالُوتَ وَجُنُودِهِۦ ۚ قَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ يَظُنُّونَ أَنَّهُم مُّلَـٰقُوا۟ ٱللَّهِ كَم مِّن فِئَةٍۢ قَلِيلَةٍ غَلَبَتْ فِئَةًۭ كَثِيرَةًۢ بِإِذْنِ ٱللَّهِ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ مَعَ ٱلصَّـٰبِرِينَ
puzzle.verse: And when Saul went forth with the soldiers, he said, "Indeed, Allāh will be testing you with a river. So whoever drinks from it is not of me, and whoever does not taste it is indeed of me, excepting one who takes [from it] in the hollow of his hand." But they drank from it, except a [very] few of them. Then when he had crossed it along with those who believed with him, they said, "There is no power for us today against Goliath and his soldiers." But those who were certain that they would meet Allāh said, "How many a small company has overcome a large company by permission of Allāh. And Allāh is with the patient." (2:249)
